skill.name: writer
skill.description: Create, convert, and automate LibreOffice Writer documents using ODT, DOCX, PDF, templates, and mail merge workflows.
skill.summary: Guides LibreOffice Writer document creation, conversion, templates, mail merge, and automation workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and network patterns, but the network, reconnaissance, and crypto indicators are false positives in documentation. The confirmed risk is that the skill encourages shell commands and Python subprocess archive operations for LibreOffice automation, which require trusted inputs and path validation. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, secret access, or data exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External Command Guidance Requires Trust Boundaries
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to run LibreOffice, package manager, archive, and process-control commands. These commands support the stated document workflow, but they can affect local files and processes if copied without reviewing paths and inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are directly present in the skill and are meant to be executed by users. The context is legitimate LibreOffice automation, so the risk is operational misuse rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Template Archive Processing Needs Input Validation
medium_findings.description: The Python template workflow extracts an archive, rewrites content.xml, and repackages output. This is useful for ODT generation, but untrusted templates or replacement values could cause unsafe file handling or malformed document content if implemented without validation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess and temporary-directory operations are explicit. The example does not show validation, but it is documentation rather than active package code, which lowers confidence in exploitability.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Benign References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to LibreOffice documentation, SDK references, API documentation, and the odfpy package page. No code in the skill sends data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are in a Resources section and are not used by executable code. No evidence found of remote requests or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Indicators Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are format conversion commands, a local LibreOffice listener, or related-skill text. No scanning, remote host enumeration, credential access, or outbound transfer is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only listener evidence is localhost LibreOffice UNO usage, and the other locations are document conversion examples. No evidence found of reconnaissance behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The flagged description line names document formats and LibreOffice Writer. It does not reference cryptography, hashing, encryption, or weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only the skill description. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell examples for LibreOffice conversion, package installation, process termination, and listener startup. These are legitimate but should be run only with trusted files and reviewed paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and relevant to the skill. They are not obfuscated or malicious, but they can modify files and local processes.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python Subprocess Archive Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The template generation example invokes external archive utilities from Python. Argument lists reduce shell injection risk, but untrusted templates and output paths still need validation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are visible and use external programs. They do not use shell=True, so command injection risk is limited but not irrelevant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Temporary Directory Document Repackaging
dangerous_patterns.description: The example creates a temporary working directory for document archive manipulation. Temporary storage is appropriate, but implementations should validate paths and clean outputs carefully.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Temporary file handling is directly shown. The sample uses TemporaryDirectory, which cleans up automatically, but archive extraction still depends on trusted inputs.

content.value_statement: Manual document formatting and conversion slow teams down. This skill guides LibreOffice Writer workflows for creation, conversion, templates, and mail merge.
actual_capabilities: Create new ODT documents using LibreOffice Writer workflows.
Convert documents between ODT, DOCX, PDF, HTML, RTF, TXT, and EPUB formats.
Guide template-based document generation with placeholder replacement.
Support mail merge planning with CSV, spreadsheet, or database sources.
Document command-line and Python approaches for Writer automation.
Provide troubleshooting steps for LibreOffice headless and UNO workflows.
limitations: Requires LibreOffice or compatible Python libraries to be installed locally.
Does not guarantee perfect formatting after every format conversion.
Template examples require input validation before use with untrusted documents.
Does not include a complete production mail merge application.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Reports
use_cases.description: Create reusable Writer templates and convert finished reports to PDF for distribution.

use_cases.title: Automate Document Conversion
use_cases.description: Convert batches of ODT, DOCX, RTF, or HTML files into required delivery formats.

use_cases.title: Generate Personalized Documents
use_cases.description: Plan mail merge workflows for letters, forms, invoices, or notices from structured data.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a LibreOffice Writer document for a one-page project update. Include a title, sections, and a clean style plan.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Document Formats
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the safest LibreOffice Writer workflow to convert an ODT document to DOCX and PDF while preserving formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Template Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Writer template workflow for monthly client reports. Include placeholders, validation checks, and output formats.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Batch Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a batch document automation plan for converting many Writer files to PDF. Include error handling, logging, and input safety checks.

output_examples.input: Create a proposal document from a reusable Writer template.
output_examples.output: A structured proposal plan with title page, section hierarchy, placeholder fields, metadata notes, and PDF export steps.

output_examples.input: Convert a folder of ODT reports to PDF.
output_examples.output: A batch conversion workflow that checks source files, converts each report, records failures, and keeps original files unchanged.

output_examples.input: Prepare a mail merge for customer letters.
output_examples.output: A mail merge outline with data fields, template placeholders, review checks, and final document export recommendations.

best_practices: Use trusted input files and review output paths before running conversion commands.
Use styles, headings, metadata, and accessibility checks in every reusable template.
Test conversions with sample documents before processing important files in bulk.
anti_patterns: Do not run batch commands on unreviewed folders or unknown document archives.
Do not rely on conversion results without checking layout, fonts, and images.
Do not insert unescaped user data into document XML templates.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create Writer documents, templates, mail merge plans, and conversion workflows for common office formats.

faq.question: Does it require LibreOffice?
faq.answer: Yes. The command-line workflows require LibreOffice, and Python workflows may require UNO or ODF libraries.

faq.question: Can it convert DOCX files to PDF?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents LibreOffice workflows for converting DOCX, ODT, and other formats to PDF.

faq.question: Can it process many documents at once?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes batch conversion guidance, but users should test commands and back up important files first.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with untrusted documents?
faq.answer: Use caution. Untrusted documents and templates should be scanned, isolated, and validated before automation.

faq.question: Does it create finished applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflows and examples that users can adapt into local automation scripts.
